edges.description: Reduced USP7 dosage perturbs PRC1-associated chromatin regulation.
Developmental regulatory dysfunction contributes to global developmental delay.
Developmental regulatory dysfunction contributes to cognitive impairment.
Cognitive dysfunction is often borderline on average, with some individuals reaching the range of intellectual disability.
Neurodevelopmental dysregulation contributes to prominent speech impairment.
Developmental regulatory dysfunction contributes to characteristic behavioral differences.
Developmental regulatory dysfunction contributes to autistic features.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to the dysmorphic phenotype.
Neurodevelopmental dysfunction can include feeding problems.
Developmental dysregulation contributes to appetite and weight abnormalities in a subset of affected individuals.

nodes.description: Dysmorphic facial features are common in Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Complex Presentation of Hao-Fountain Syndrome Solved by Exome Sequencing Highlighting Co-Occurring Genomic Variants.
evidence.snippet: A 20-year-old woman affected by moderate intellectual disability (ID), dysmorphic features, hypertrichosis, scoliosis, recurrent bronchitis, and pneumonia with bronchiectasis, colelithiasis, chronic severe constipation, and a family history suggestive of autosomal dominant recurrence of polycystic kidney disease was analyzed by WES to identify the genomic events underlying the condition.

nodes.description: Autism spectrum disorder is part of the core neurobehavioral phenotype.
Distinctive behavioral abnormalities are part of the Hao-Fountain syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Complex Presentation of Hao-Fountain Syndrome Solved by Exome Sequencing Highlighting Co-Occurring Genomic Variants.
evidence.snippet: A de novo truncating USP7 variant was disclosed as the cause of Hao-Fountain syndrome, a disorder characterized by syndromic ID and distinctive behavior.

nodes.description: Cognitive function is often borderline on average in Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Hao-Fountain syndrome: 32 novel patients reveal new insights into the clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Cognitive testing reports reveal borderline intellectual functioning on average, although some individuals score in the range of intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Speech impairment is a major and potentially hallmark feature of Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Hao-Fountain syndrome: 32 novel patients reveal new insights into the clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Speech impairment emerges as a potential hallmark of Hao-Fountain syndrome.

nodes.description: Feeding problems are part of the broader developmental presentation.
Global developmental delay is a central early neurodevelopmental manifestation of Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.snippet: global developmental delay, intellectual disability, severe speech delay

nodes.description: Hyperphagia with increased body weight is reported in a subset of individuals with Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Hao-Fountain syndrome: 32 novel patients reveal new insights into the clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: In addition to confirming previously described features, we report hyperphagia and increased body weight in a subset of individuals.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is reported among additional Hao-Fountain syndrome features.
Cognitive dysfunction ranges from borderline intellectual functioning to intellectual disability in Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Complex Presentation of Hao-Fountain Syndrome Solved by Exome Sequencing Highlighting Co-Occurring Genomic Variants.
evidence.snippet: A 20-year-old woman affected by moderate intellectual disability (ID), dysmorphic features, hypertrichosis, scoliosis, recurrent bronchitis, and pneumonia with bronchiectasis, colelithiasis, chronic severe constipation, and a family history suggestive of autosomal dominant recurrence of polycystic kidney disease was analyzed by WES to identify the genomic events underlying the condition.

evidence.reference_title: Hao-Fountain syndrome: 32 novel patients reveal new insights into the clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Cognitive testing reports reveal borderline intellectual functioning on average, although some individuals score in the range of intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Hao-Fountain syndrome shows methylation and expression abnormalities within PRC1-associated chromatin domains and developmental genes.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: Integrative epigenetic and transcriptomic profiling of whole blood and fibroblasts in Hao-Fountain syndrome
evidence.snippet: Fibroblasts revealed coherent methylation and expression changes at developmental genes, whereas blood captured the diagnostic episignature and a smaller set of downstream regulatory alterations.

nodes.description: Seizures are reported in a subset of individuals with Hao-Fountain syndrome.

evidence.snippet: hypogonadism in males, seizures and ocular anomalies

evidence.reference_title: Complex Presentation of Hao-Fountain Syndrome Solved by Exome Sequencing Highlighting Co-Occurring Genomic Variants.
evidence.snippet: A de novo truncating USP7 variant was disclosed as the cause of Hao-Fountain syndrome, a disorder characterized by syndromic ID and distinctive behavior.
USP7 | HGNC:12630 | Hao-Fountain syndrome | MONDO:0014805 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Reduced USP7 dosage is the initiating molecular lesion in Hao-Fountain syndrome and perturbs several cellular homeostatic mechanisms, including functions linked to the MUST complex.

evidence.reference_title: Integrative epigenetic and transcriptomic profiling of whole blood and fibroblasts in Hao-Fountain syndrome
evidence.snippet: USP7 haploinsufficiency is associated with a restricted set of regulatory loci enriched within PRC1-associated chromatin domains.

evidence.reference_title: Hao-Fountain syndrome: 32 novel patients reveal new insights into the clinical spectrum.
evidence.snippet: Hao-Fountain syndrome (HAFOUS, OMIM:  # 616863) is a neurodevelopmental disorder caused by pathogenic variants in the gene USP7 coding for USP7, a protein involved in several crucial cellular homeostatic mechanisms and the recently described MUST complex.
